articles.headline: Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha Witness Heated Debate on 'Operation Sindoor' Amidst Opposition Protests on Bihar's Special Intensive Rolls Revision
articles.explainer:
• Parliamentarians engaged in a special discussion regarding 'Operation Sindoor' and its implications for the electoral process.
• Opposition parties staged protests, expressing concerns over alleged irregularities and political motivations behind the Special Intensive Rolls Revision in Bihar.
• The debate highlighted issues of federalism, electoral integrity, and the role of central government interventions in state-level electoral administration.
• Concerns were raised about the transparency and fairness of the voter registration process, potentially disenfranchising segments of the population.
• The government defended the operation as a necessary measure to ensure accurate and updated voter lists, crucial for democratic elections.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures
Government policies and interventions
Federalism
Electoral reforms
Issues arising out of design and implementation of policies
prelimsPoints: Operation Sindoor refers to a specific initiative, reportedly related to electoral roll revision in Bihar.
The discussion occurred simultaneously in both houses of the Indian Parliament: Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.
Special Intensive Rolls Revision is a process to update and verify voter lists.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges of maintaining electoral integrity in India, especially concerning voter roll revisions and potential political interference.
Analyze the role of parliamentary oversight and opposition protests in holding the government accountable for policies affecting democratic processes.
Examine the delicate balance of federal relations when central initiatives intersect with state administration of elections.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of the Indian Parliament building with protesting politicians outside
articles.trivia: The Election Commission of India is an autonomous constitutional authority responsible for administering election processes in India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges

articles.headline: Hindi vs. Marathi: Thackeray Factions Unite on Language Protection, Signaling Maharashtra's Shifting Political Landscape
articles.explainer:
• Uddhav Thackeray and Raj Thackeray shared a public stage after 19 years, brought together by concerns over the perceived threat to Marathi language and culture.
• The reunion was reportedly prompted by the BJP's language experiments and policies, seen as promoting Hindi at the expense of regional languages.
• This unexpected unity could reshape political alliances in Maharashtra, impacting upcoming state and national elections.
• Language politics continues to be a potent force in Indian federalism, often triggering regional sentiments and political realignments.
• Deputy Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis's actions were cited as a catalyst for the Thackerays to overcome their long-standing differences.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Regionalism
Political parties and pressure groups
prelimsPoints: Uddhav Thackeray and Raj Thackeray are prominent political figures in Maharashtra, leading different factions of the Shiv Sena.
The event marks their first shared public platform in 19 years.
The issue at hand is the protection of the Marathi language and culture against alleged Hindi imposition.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of language in shaping regional identities and political movements in India, using the Thackeray reunion as a case study.
Discuss the challenges posed by linguistic nationalism to India's federal structure and national unity.
Evaluate the impact of such political realignments on state-level governance and the broader national political landscape.
articles.imageDescription: Uddhav Thackeray and Raj Thackeray shaking hands on a stage with a Marathi flag in the background.
articles.trivia: Maharashtra Day, celebrated on May 1st, commemorates the formation of the state of Maharashtra from the erstwhile Bombay State on linguistic grounds.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure

articles.headline: Netanyahu’s Military Successes Come at a Dangerous Cost: Israel’s Unchecked Aggression Deepens Regional Fault Lines and Its Own Vulnerabilities
articles.explainer:
• Israel's recent military operations, while achieving tactical successes, have exacerbated existing tensions across the Middle East.
• Critics argue that the aggressive stance has deepened regional fault lines, making long-term peace and stability more elusive.
• The article highlights that Israel's unchecked aggression is paradoxically increasing its own vulnerabilities, potentially leading to prolonged conflict.
• The humanitarian cost and displacement of populations in conflict zones have drawn widespread international condemnation.
• Regional powers and international bodies express concern over the escalating violence and its potential to destabilize the broader geopolitical landscape.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora
Conflict zones and their resolution
prelimsPoints: Benjamin Netanyahu is the Prime Minister of Israel.
The article refers to recent military successes and their long-term regional impact.
The Middle East is a significant region for global geopolitical stability.
mainsPoints: Analyze how Israel's military strategies, while achieving short-term objectives, might be counterproductive to long-term regional stability and its own security interests.
Discuss the role of international bodies and global powers in mediating conflicts in the Middle East and the challenges they face.
Examine the implications of escalating conflicts in West Asia for India's foreign policy and energy security.
articles.imageDescription: Map of the Middle East showing conflict zones and regional powers.
articles.trivia: The term 'Middle East' gained prominence in the early 20th century, largely replacing the 'Near East' to describe the region.

articles.headline: Israel’s ‘Total War’ Wrecks the Axis of Resistance but Not Its Logic; Lasting Peace Demands a Viable Palestinian State
articles.explainer:
• Israel's recent 'total war' strategy has significantly disrupted the operational capabilities of various groups constituting the 'Axis of Resistance'.
• Despite military setbacks, the underlying ideological logic and shared goals of these groups are expected to reconfigure and persist.
• The article argues that a lasting and sustainable peace in the region is contingent upon the establishment of a viable Palestinian state.
• Iran and allied groups are predicted to adapt through informal economies and renewed ideological solidarity, rather than being dismantled.
• International efforts for a two-state solution are highlighted as crucial, with current military actions perceived as delaying rather than resolving the core issue.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
India and its neighbourhood- relations
Conflict zones and their resolution
prelimsPoints: The 'Axis of Resistance' is a loose alliance of states and non-state actors in the Middle East, led by Iran, opposing Israeli and Western influence.
A viable Palestinian state is a key component of the proposed 'two-state solution' for the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.
Iran plays a central role in supporting several groups within the Axis of Resistance.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of military approaches in resolving deeply entrenched ideological conflicts, specifically in the context of the Israeli-Palestinian issue.
Discuss the concept of the 'Axis of Resistance' and its evolving nature in response to geopolitical pressures and military actions.
Argue for the imperative of a viable Palestinian state in achieving lasting peace and stability in the Middle East, considering various international perspectives.
articles.imageDescription: Stylized map showing the influence of the 'Axis of Resistance' in the Middle East.
articles.trivia: The concept of a 'two-state solution' emerged prominently after the 1967 Six-Day War, advocating for an independent Palestinian state alongside Israel.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Conflict resolution and international peace efforts

articles.headline: Israel’s 12-Day War Leaves Iran on a Security-First Path, Accelerating Shift from Revolutionary Idealism to Strategic Pragmatism
articles.explainer:
• The recent 12-day conflict involving Israel has significantly impacted Iran's foreign policy calculus and internal dynamics.
• The war has deepened Iran’s international isolation, forcing a re-evaluation of its regional and global strategies.
• Internally, the conflict has revived a strong nationalist core within Iran, potentially shifting focus from revolutionary ideals to national security.
• This shift indicates a move towards strategic pragmatism, prioritizing national interests and security over ideological expansionism.
• The article suggests that Iran's future actions will be more calculated and defensive, aiming to consolidate power and protect its borders amidst regional volatility.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
India and its neighbourhood- relations
Geopolitics of West Asia
prelimsPoints: Iran's foreign policy has historically been influenced by its revolutionary ideals since 1979.
The article discusses a shift towards 'strategic pragmatism' in Iran's foreign policy.
The 12-day war refers to a specific recent conflict involving Israel, impacting Iran.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors driving Iran's shift from revolutionary idealism to strategic pragmatism in its foreign policy, and its implications for regional stability.
Discuss how external conflicts and international isolation can reshape a nation's internal political dynamics and foster nationalism.
Examine the potential impact of Iran's evolving foreign policy on India's strategic interests, particularly in energy security and regional connectivity.
articles.imageDescription: Iranian flag alongside a map of the Gulf region, symbolizing its strategic focus.
articles.trivia: The Iranian Revolution of 1979 transformed Iran from a monarchy into an Islamic republic, profoundly impacting its geopolitical stance.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Foreign policy shifts of key regional players

articles.headline: New Literary Releases Explore Kerala’s Naxalite Movement, Invisible Digital Labor, and Hindi Translations, Offering Diverse Perspectives
articles.explainer:
• A new novel delves into the historical context and human stories behind Kerala’s Naxalite movement, shedding light on its complexities.
• Another book examines the often-invisible lives and labor that underpin our modern digital ease, revealing the human cost of technology.
• The literary landscape is enriched by new translations from Hindi, including works by Siyaram Sharan Gupta, making diverse Indian voices accessible.
• These books contribute to a broader understanding of India's socio-political history, contemporary challenges, and rich literary heritage.
• The releases highlight the growing trend of exploring niche historical movements and the ethical dimensions of technological advancements through literature.
gsSubTopics: Post-independence consolidation and reorganization within the country
Salient features of Indian Society
Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
prelimsPoints: Naxalite movement originated in Naxalbari, West Bengal, in 1967 and spread to various parts of India, including Kerala.
Siyaram Sharan Gupta is a noted Hindi author.
The books cover diverse topics from historical movements to contemporary social issues related to technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of literature in re-interpreting historical events like the Naxalite movement and providing deeper societal insights.
Examine the ethical and social implications of the 'invisible labor' that supports the digital economy, as explored in contemporary literature.
Highlight the significance of literary translations in fostering cultural exchange and making regional narratives accessible to a wider audience.
articles.imageDescription: Stack of newly released books with diverse covers, one depicting a rural Kerala scene.
articles.trivia: The Naxalite movement in Kerala, though less widespread than in other states, saw significant activity in regions like Wayanad and Kannur in the late 1960s and early 1970s.

articles.headline: National Research Foundation Gains Momentum: Government Announces New Funding Tranches for Interdisciplinary Research and Development
articles.explainer:
• The government has released fresh tranches of funding for the National Research Foundation (NRF), reinforcing its commitment to scientific advancement.
• These funds are earmarked to promote interdisciplinary research, fostering collaboration across various scientific and technological domains.
• The NRF aims to strengthen India's research ecosystem, driving innovation and enhancing global competitiveness in science and technology.
• Focus areas include emerging technologies, sustainable development, health sciences, and fundamental research.
• The initiative is expected to boost private sector participation and attract top talent to research careers, aligning with the National Education Policy (NEP).
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: The National Research Foundation (NRF) was established to fund and promote research across various disciplines in India.
It aims to bridge the gap between academic research and industrial application.
The NRF operates under the Department of Science & Technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and potential impact of the National Research Foundation (NRF) in transforming India's scientific research landscape.
Analyze the importance of interdisciplinary research in addressing complex national and global challenges, citing examples of NRF's focus areas.
Examine the role of government funding and policy interventions in fostering a robust innovation ecosystem and promoting 'Make in India' in science and technology.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic representing interdisciplinary research connecting various scientific fields.
articles.trivia: The idea for a National Research Foundation was initially proposed in the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government policies for science and technology

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs Government to Expedite Rules for Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Emphasizing Citizen Privacy Rights
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has issued directives to the Union Government regarding the swift formulation and notification of rules under the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023.
• The ruling underscores the judiciary's commitment to safeguarding citizen privacy rights in the digital age.
• Delays in framing rules have created a regulatory vacuum, impacting data fiduciaries and data principals across various sectors.
• The court emphasized the need for clarity and enforceability to effectively implement the provisions of the DPDP Act.
• This decision is crucial for protecting individuals' personal data and fostering trust in India's digital economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Role of Supreme Court
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is India's comprehensive law on data privacy.
The Supreme Court's directives are aimed at ensuring timely implementation of the Act.
The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy judgment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, in the context of India's growing digital economy and the fundamental right to privacy.
Analyze the challenges involved in framing and implementing rules for complex legislation like data protection, considering stakeholder interests and technological advancements.
Examine the role of the judiciary in ensuring the timely and effective implementation of laws and protecting citizen rights against executive delays.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building facade, symbolizing judicial intervention.
articles.trivia: The concept of a separate data protection law for India gained prominence after the Justice B.N. Srikrishna Committee's recommendations in 2018.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and data protection laws

articles.headline: ISRO's Aditya-L1 Continues Solar Observations, Providing Crucial Data on Coronal Mass Ejections and Space Weather
articles.explainer:
• India's first solar observatory mission, Aditya-L1, continues to successfully orbit the Sun-Earth L1 point, actively gathering data.
• The spacecraft is providing unprecedented insights into solar phenomena, including Coronal Mass Ejections (CMEs) and solar flares.
• The data is crucial for understanding space weather, which can impact Earth's satellites, communication systems, and power grids.
• Aditya-L1's instruments are designed to study the solar corona, chromosphere, and solar wind, contributing significantly to heliophysics.
• The mission highlights India's growing capabilities in advanced space research and its contribution to global scientific endeavors.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: Aditya-L1 is India's first dedicated solar mission.
It is positioned in a halo orbit around the Sun-Earth Lagrange Point 1 (L1).
Coronal Mass Ejections (CMEs) are large expulsions of plasma and magnetic field from the Sun's corona.
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific objectives and key contributions of ISRO's Aditya-L1 mission to the field of heliophysics and space weather prediction.
Analyze the significance of understanding space weather for protecting critical infrastructure on Earth and in space.
Evaluate India's advancements in space technology and its role as a global leader in space exploration, citing missions like Aditya-L1.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendition of the Aditya-L1 spacecraft orbiting at the L1 point, observing the Sun.
articles.trivia: Lagrange Points are positions in space where the gravitational forces of two large bodies (like the Sun and Earth) balance, allowing a smaller object to maintain a stable position relative to them.

articles.headline: IMD Forecasts Normal Monsoon for Key Agricultural Regions, Raising Hopes for Robust Kharif Output and Rural Demand
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a favorable forecast for the monsoon season, predicting normal rainfall across major agricultural zones.
• This forecast has significantly boosted optimism for a robust Kharif crop output, crucial for India's food security.
• A strong monsoon is expected to enhance rural incomes and stimulate rural demand, contributing positively to overall economic growth.
• Timely and adequate rainfall is vital for irrigation, replenishing groundwater levels, and ensuring agricultural productivity.
• However, regional variations and the potential for extreme weather events remain concerns that require continuous monitoring.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
Land reforms in India.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: Kharif crops are monsoon crops, typically sown with the onset of the monsoon (June-July) and harvested in September-October.
IMD is the primary agency responsible for meteorological observations, weather forecasting, and seismology in India.
A 'normal monsoon' is defined based on long-period average (LPA) rainfall.
mainsPoints: Analyze the critical dependence of the Indian economy on the monsoon, highlighting its impact on agriculture, rural livelihoods, and inflation.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in Indian agriculture, considering climate change, irrigation infrastructure, and government support mechanisms.
Examine the policy measures required to mitigate the risks associated with monsoon variability and ensure long-term food security for India.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers working in a lush green paddy field during the monsoon season.
articles.trivia: The Indian monsoon is driven by the differential heating of land and sea, leading to a seasonal reversal of winds.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Monsoon and its impact on agriculture

articles.headline: National Wetland Conservation Programme Launched to Protect Critical Ecosystems, Emphasizing Community Participation and Sustainable Use
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has launched a revamped National Wetland Conservation Programme (NWCP).
• The program aims to protect and restore India's vital wetland ecosystems, which are crucial for biodiversity and ecological balance.
• A key focus is on involving local communities in conservation efforts, promoting sustainable livelihoods and traditional knowledge.
• The initiative includes measures for wetland inventory and assessment, capacity building, and enforcement against encroachment and pollution.
• Wetlands provide essential ecosystem services, including water purification, flood control, and habitat for diverse flora and fauna.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Ecosystems
articles.source: Ministry of Environment / Down To Earth
prelimsPoints: Wetlands are areas where water covers the soil, or is present either at or near the surface of the soil all year or for varying periods of time.
India is a signatory to the Ramsar Convention on Wetlands.
The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change is the nodal ministry for wetland conservation in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of wetlands and the various threats they face in India, such as pollution, encroachment, and climate change.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives like the National Wetland Conservation Programme in achieving conservation goals, emphasizing community involvement.
Suggest sustainable management practices and policy reforms necessary for the long-term protection and wise use of India's wetland resources.
articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds and lush vegetation.
articles.trivia: The Chilika Lake in Odisha is Asia's largest brackish water lagoon and was the first Indian wetland designated as a Ramsar site.

articles.headline: India Gears Up for Upcoming India-Africa Forum Summit, Focusing on Enhanced Cooperation in Trade, Technology, and Capacity Building
articles.explainer:
• Preparations are underway for the next India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS), signaling India's continued strategic outreach to the African continent.
• The summit aims to bolster cooperation across key sectors including trade, investment, technology transfer, and human resource development.
• India emphasizes a partnership approach based on mutual respect, shared growth, and South-South cooperation, distinct from traditional donor-recipient models.
• Discussions will likely cover global issues such as climate change, counter-terrorism, and reforms of multilateral institutions.
• The summit underscores Africa's growing geopolitical and economic importance and India's commitment to strengthening ties with African nations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: The India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) is the official platform for India-Africa relations.
Key pillars of cooperation often include trade, investment, technology, and human resource development.
India's engagement with Africa is based on a model of South-South cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of India's engagement with Africa, outlining the key areas of cooperation and its 'South-South' approach.
Discuss how the India-Africa Forum Summit contributes to India's foreign policy objectives and its aspiration for a greater role in global governance.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in deepening India-Africa relations, considering geopolitical shifts and the presence of other global powers in Africa.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and various African nations in a conference setting.
articles.trivia: The first India-Africa Forum Summit was held in New Delhi in 2008, marking a new era of strategic partnership.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Progress on National Mental Healthcare Programme, Addressing Gaps in Access and Stigma Reduction
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare conducted a review of the National Mental Healthcare Programme (NMHP) to assess its implementation.
• The review identified persistent gaps in the accessibility of mental health services, particularly in rural and remote areas.
• Efforts to reduce the pervasive social stigma associated with mental illness were highlighted as crucial for encouraging help-seeking behavior.
• The government plans to strengthen infrastructure, increase the workforce of mental health professionals, and integrate mental health into primary healthcare.
• This initiative is vital for addressing the growing burden of mental health disorders and ensuring holistic well-being for all citizens.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: The National Mental Healthcare Programme (NMHP) was launched in 1982 to provide mental health services.
The Mental Healthcare Act, 2017, decriminalized suicide and provides for rights of persons with mental illness.
Integration of mental health services into primary healthcare is a key strategy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in providing comprehensive mental healthcare in India, focusing on issues of access, affordability, and social stigma.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives like the National Mental Healthcare Programme and the Mental Healthcare Act, 2017, in addressing mental health concerns.
Suggest innovative strategies and policy recommendations for strengthening India's mental healthcare infrastructure and promoting mental well-being across all sections of society.
articles.imageDescription: Symbolic image of a brain with intertwined hands, representing mental health support.
articles.trivia: Globally, one in eight people live with a mental disorder, and India accounts for a significant portion of this burden.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry Unveils New Incentives to Boost Indigenous Production of Critical Military Hardware Under 'Make in India' Initiative
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence has announced a new package of incentives aimed at accelerating indigenous production of military hardware.
• This move is part of the broader 'Make in India' initiative, seeking to enhance self-reliance in the defence sector and reduce import dependence.
• The incentives include preferential procurement, increased funding for R&D, and streamlined approval processes for domestic manufacturers.
• The focus is on critical technologies and systems, ranging from advanced weaponry to surveillance equipment and communication systems.
• Boosting indigenous defence production is crucial for national security, economic growth, and job creation in the manufacturing sector.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Defence technology and indigenization.
prelimsPoints: The 'Make in India' initiative aims to encourage companies to manufacture in India and incentivize dedicated investments.
India is among the largest importers of defence equipment globally.
Defence corridors have been established in Uttar Pradesh and Tamil Nadu to boost defence manufacturing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic imperative of achieving self-reliance in defence production for India's national security and geopolitical standing.
Analyze the various incentives and policy measures adopted by the government to boost the 'Make in India' initiative in the defence sector, highlighting their potential impact.
Examine the challenges faced by indigenous defence manufacturers and suggest further steps to foster a vibrant domestic defence industrial base.
articles.imageDescription: A modern Indian tank being assembled in a factory, symbolizing indigenous production.
articles.trivia: India aims to achieve a turnover of US$25 billion in defence production, including exports of US$5 billion, by 2025.

articles.headline: Smart Cities Mission Enters New Phase, Focusing on Sustainable Urban Planning, Climate Resilience, and Citizen-Centric Services
articles.explainer:
• The Smart Cities Mission (SCM) has entered its next phase, building upon the lessons learned from the initial implementation.
• The renewed focus is on integrating sustainable urban planning principles, emphasizing green infrastructure and resource efficiency.
• Climate resilience is a major theme, with projects aimed at mitigating and adapting to the impacts of climate change in urban areas.
• Emphasis is placed on delivering citizen-centric services through technology, improving quality of life and urban governance.
• The mission seeks to transform urban landscapes into more livable, economically vibrant, and environmentally sustainable spaces.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: The Smart Cities Mission was launched by the Indian government in 2015.
Its objective is to promote cities that provide core infrastructure, a clean and sustainable environment, and a decent quality of life to their citizens.
The mission covers 100 cities across India.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the progress and challenges of the Smart Cities Mission in India, particularly in its new phase focusing on sustainability and climate resilience.
Discuss how technology and citizen participation can enhance urban governance and improve the delivery of essential services in smart cities.
Examine the role of sustainable urban planning in addressing the growing challenges of urbanization, environmental degradation, and climate change in India.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic city skyline with green spaces and smart infrastructure elements.
articles.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission is based on the concept of 'area-based development', which includes retrofitting, redevelopment, and greenfield development.

articles.headline: Recent Archaeological Excavations in Harappan Sites Uncover New Insights into Indus Valley Civilization’s Trade Networks
articles.explainer:
• Ongoing archaeological excavations at various Harappan sites have yielded significant findings related to ancient trade routes.
• New evidence suggests extensive trade networks connecting the Indus Valley Civilization with distant regions, including Mesopotamia and Central Asia.
• Artifacts such as seals, pottery, and raw materials found at distant sites provide clues about the commodities exchanged and the nature of these interactions.
• The discoveries shed light on the sophisticated economic organization and maritime capabilities of the Harappan people.
• These findings contribute to a deeper understanding of the Harappan civilization's influence and its integration into early global trade systems.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Ancient Indian history
Indus Valley Civilization
articles.source: Archaeological Survey of India / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: The Indus Valley Civilization (also known as Harappan Civilization) flourished around 2500-1900 BCE.
Major Harappan sites include Harappa, Mohenjo-Daro, Lothal, Dholavira, and Rakhigarhi.
Mesopotamia was a contemporary civilization with which the Harappans had trade relations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in re-interpreting the economic and cultural interactions of the Indus Valley Civilization with other ancient civilizations.
Analyze the features of Harappan trade networks, including commodities exchanged, modes of transport, and the role of specific sites like Lothal.
Examine how archaeological evidence helps reconstruct the social, economic, and political structures of ancient civilizations like the Harappan.
articles.imageDescription: Reconstruction of a Harappan city with trade boats in a dockyard.
articles.trivia: Lothal, located in Gujarat, is believed to have been a major port city of the Indus Valley Civilization, featuring a well-planned dockyard.

articles.headline: India Pushes for Enhanced Climate Finance Commitments at G20 Meeting, Emphasizing Equity and Common but Differentiated Responsibilities
articles.explainer:
• India advocated for stronger commitments on climate finance from developed nations during a recent G20 Finance Ministers' meeting.
• The focus was on the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in climate action.
• Developing nations require significant financial and technological support to meet their climate goals and transition to green economies.
• India highlighted the need for accessible and affordable climate finance, moving beyond pledges to concrete delivery mechanisms.
• The discussions underscored the global challenge of funding climate mitigation and adaptation efforts, especially for vulnerable economies.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Climate Change and International Forums
articles.source: Ministry of Finance / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: G20 (Group of Twenty) is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
CBDR-RC is a principle in international environmental law, recognizing different capabilities and historical responsibilities of countries.
Climate finance refers to local, national or transnational financing, drawn from public, private and alternative sources of financing, that seeks to support mitigation and adaptation actions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of climate finance in achieving global climate goals and the challenges faced by developing nations in accessing adequate funding.
Analyze India's stance on climate finance at international forums like G20, emphasizing the principle of CBDR-RC and its implications for global equity.
Examine the mechanisms for delivering climate finance and suggest ways to improve their effectiveness and transparency for vulnerable economies.
articles.imageDescription: A globe with hands symbolizing international cooperation and climate finance flowing between nations.
articles.trivia: The Paris Agreement (2015) explicitly mentions the need for developed countries to provide financial resources to assist developing countries with respect to both mitigation and adaptation.

articles.headline: RBI Holds Key Policy Rates Steady in Monetary Policy Review, Citing Inflationary Concerns and Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) decided to maintain the repo rate and other key policy rates unchanged in its latest Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) review.
• The decision was primarily driven by persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food and energy sectors.
• The RBI also cited global economic uncertainties and geopolitical tensions as significant headwinds impacting domestic growth.
• This stance indicates a cautious approach to monetary policy, prioritizing price stability while supporting economic growth.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from the central bank, with future rate actions dependent on evolving inflation and growth dynamics.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory body of the RBI responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate.
The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation targeting is a key mandate of the RBI, aiming to keep Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation within a target range.
mainsPoints: Discuss the primary objectives of the RBI's monetary policy and the rationale behind holding key policy rates steady amidst current economic conditions.
Analyze the interplay between inflation, economic growth, and global economic factors in shaping monetary policy decisions in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various monetary policy tools used by the RBI in achieving its dual objectives of price stability and sustainable growth.
articles.imageDescription: The RBI logo prominently displayed, symbolizing monetary authority.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 under the RBI Act, 1934, with six members: three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.

articles.headline: National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Gains Pace with Focus on Foundational Literacy and Numeracy Across States
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education reports significant progress in the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• A key thrust is on strengthening Foundational Literacy and Numeracy (FLN) skills among young learners through various state-level initiatives.
• The policy aims to transform India's education system from early childhood to higher education, promoting holistic and multidisciplinary learning.
• Efforts include curriculum reforms, teacher training programs, and leveraging technology for improved learning outcomes.
• Challenges persist in ensuring equitable access and quality education across diverse socio-economic backgrounds, requiring sustained collaborative efforts.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Education / The Indian Express
prelimsPoints: The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the 1986 policy.
Foundational Literacy and Numeracy (FLN) is a core component of NEP, aimed at achieving universal FLN by 2025.
The NIPUN Bharat Mission is a national initiative to achieve the goals of FLN.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in addressing the challenges of India's education system.
Analyze the importance of Foundational Literacy and Numeracy (FLN) for overall educational development and its role in achieving NEP's objectives.
Examine the challenges in implementing a nationwide education policy like NEP, considering federal structure, resource allocation, and diverse regional needs.
articles.imageDescription: Children in a classroom actively engaged in learning activities, symbolizing foundational education.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 recommends a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure, replacing the 10+2 structure.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government policies and programs in education

highlights: Parliament debates 'Operation Sindoor' and Bihar's electoral rolls, raising concerns over transparency and federalism.
Thackeray factions unite in Maharashtra on language protection, signaling a shift in state politics amidst BJP's policies.
Israel's military actions deepen regional fault lines, with calls for a viable Palestinian state intensifying for lasting peace.
RBI holds key policy rates steady, prioritizing inflation control amidst global economic uncertainties and domestic price pressures.
National Research Foundation receives new funding tranches, boosting interdisciplinary R&D and India's scientific innovation ecosystem.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent debate on 'Operation Sindoor' and Special Intensive Rolls Revision in Bihar, consider the following statements:
1. 'Operation Sindoor' is a security operation aimed at combating left-wing extremism.
2. The Special Intensive Rolls Revision is a process conducted by the Election Commission of India to update voter lists.
3. The Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha discussed the matter simultaneously, indicating its national importance.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect as 'Operation Sindoor' in this context refers to a special discussion on the Special Intensive Rolls Revision, which is related to electoral processes, not explicitly a security operation against left-wing extremism. Statement 2 is correct; Special Intensive Rolls Revision is indeed a process for updating voter lists, overseen by the Election Commission. Statement 3 is correct, as per the news, a special discussion on the matter was held in both houses of Parliament.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the primary objective of India's Aditya-L1 mission?
options: (a) To study the surface composition of Mars.
(b) To explore the Moon's south pole for water ice.
(c) To observe the Sun's corona and study space weather.
(d) To search for exoplanets in distant star systems.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) To observe the Sun's corona and study space weather.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Aditya-L1 is India's first dedicated solar mission. Its primary objective is to study the Sun's outer layers (corona, chromosphere), solar emissions, solar winds, coronal mass ejections (CMEs), and their impact on space weather. Options (a) and (b) relate to other planetary missions, and (d) relates to exoplanet research, which are not the objectives of Aditya-L1.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following about the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023:
1. It mandates data localization for all personal data collected in India.
2. The Supreme Court has recently directed the government to expedite the framing of its rules.
3. It recognizes the 'Right to be Forgotten' as a fundamental right.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the DPDP Act, 2023, does not mandate data localization for all personal data, offering greater flexibility compared to earlier drafts. Statement 2 is correct, as per the news. Statement 3 is incorrect; while the 'Right to be Forgotten' is a concept discussed in data protection, the DPDP Act specifically provides for the 'Right to Erasure' or 'Right to Correction and Erasure' under certain conditions, which is similar but not explicitly termed 'Right to be Forgotten' as a standalone fundamental right within the Act's provisions.

prelimsMocks.question: The term 'Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)', often seen in news, is associated with which of the following?
prelimsMocks.explanation: CBDR-RC is a principle within international environmental law, particularly prominent in climate change negotiations. It acknowledges that all states have a shared responsibility to address environmental degradation but recognizes their differing contributions to the problem and their respective economic and technical capacities to tackle it.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements regarding the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 is/are correct?
1. It proposes a 10+2 curricular structure for school education.
2. A key focus area is strengthening Foundational Literacy and Numeracy (FLN) skills.
3. It aims to achieve universal FLN by 2030.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the NEP 2020 proposes a new 5+3+3+4 curricular structure, replacing the 10+2 structure. Statement 2 is correct, as strengthening Foundational Literacy and Numeracy (FLN) is a core component. Statement 3 is incorrect; the NEP 2020 and its related initiatives (like NIPUN Bharat) aim to achieve universal FLN by 2025, not 2030.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze how language politics and regional identities impact the federal structure and political alliances in India, with specific reference to the recent developments in Maharashtra. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, political parties.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Thackeray reunion as a case of language-driven political realignment. Discuss how language can unite factions despite long-standing differences. Explain the implications for federalism (state vs. center on language policy) and for state politics (new alliances, vote bank shifts). Conclude on the enduring power of regional identity in India's political landscape.

mainsMocks.question: Evaluate the effectiveness of India's 'Make in India' initiative in achieving self-reliance in the defence sector. What further steps are needed to foster a robust domestic defence industrial base? (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Defence technology; Indigenization of technology; Internal Security.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by defining 'Make in India' in defence and its strategic importance (reducing import dependence, job creation, strategic autonomy). Discuss successes (e.g., increased indigenous procurement, defence corridors, private sector entry). Highlight challenges (e.g., R&D gaps, lack of critical technology, procedural delays, funding). Suggest further steps: increased R&D investment, technology transfer mechanisms, long-term procurement visibility, skill development, export promotion, and streamlined regulatory processes.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's directives on the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, underscore the judiciary's role in safeguarding citizen privacy. Discuss the significance of this Act and the challenges in its effective implementation in India. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Fundamental Rights, Government policies and interventions, Role of Supreme Court. GS3: Cyber security, IT Act.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the DPDP Act, 2023, as India's comprehensive data privacy law and the SC's recent intervention. Explain the Act's significance (protecting fundamental right to privacy, fostering trust in digital economy, regulating data fiduciaries). Discuss implementation challenges: framing detailed rules, capacity building for Data Protection Board, balancing innovation with privacy, cross-border data flow issues, public awareness, enforcement mechanisms, and dealing with legacy data. Conclude on the need for robust regulatory framework and continuous adaptation.

quickBytes.text: The 12th India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) is slated to enhance cooperation in trade, technology, and capacity building.
The National Mental Healthcare Programme (NMHP) was launched in India in 1982.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is India's first comprehensive law on data privacy.
The National Research Foundation (NRF) aims to boost R&D across disciplines and is linked to NEP 2020.
Aditya-L1, India's solar mission, is placed in a halo orbit around the Sun-Earth Lagrange Point 1 (L1).
Kharif crops are monsoon crops, sown in June-July and harvested in September-October.
The Ramsar Convention is an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of RBI has six members: three from RBI and three external.
The Harappan civilization was known for its well-planned cities, sophisticated drainage systems, and extensive trade networks.
The 'Make in India' initiative in defence aims to reduce import dependence and boost indigenous production.

GS1.headline: New Literary Releases Explore Kerala’s Naxalite Movement, Invisible Digital Labor, and Hindi Translations, Offering Diverse Perspectives
GS1.explainer:
• A new novel delves into the historical context and human stories behind Kerala’s Naxalite movement, shedding light on its complexities.
• Another book examines the often-invisible lives and labor that underpin our modern digital ease, revealing the human cost of technology.
• The literary landscape is enriched by new translations from Hindi, including works by Siyaram Sharan Gupta, making diverse Indian voices accessible.
• These books contribute to a broader understanding of India's socio-political history, contemporary challenges, and rich literary heritage.
• The releases highlight the growing trend of exploring niche historical movements and the ethical dimensions of technological advancements through literature.
gsSubTopics: Post-independence consolidation and reorganization within the country
Salient features of Indian Society
Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
prelimsPoints: Naxalite movement originated in Naxalbari, West Bengal, in 1967 and spread to various parts of India, including Kerala.
Siyaram Sharan Gupta is a noted Hindi author.
The books cover diverse topics from historical movements to contemporary social issues related to technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of literature in re-interpreting historical events like the Naxalite movement and providing deeper societal insights.
Examine the ethical and social implications of the 'invisible labor' that supports the digital economy, as explored in contemporary literature.
Highlight the significance of literary translations in fostering cultural exchange and making regional narratives accessible to a wider audience.
GS1.imageDescription: Stack of newly released books with diverse covers, one depicting a rural Kerala scene.
GS1.trivia: The Naxalite movement in Kerala, though less widespread than in other states, saw significant activity in regions like Wayanad and Kannur in the late 1960s and early 1970s.

GS1.headline: Recent Archaeological Excavations in Harappan Sites Uncover New Insights into Indus Valley Civilization’s Trade Networks
GS1.explainer:
• Ongoing archaeological excavations at various Harappan sites have yielded significant findings related to ancient trade routes.
• New evidence suggests extensive trade networks connecting the Indus Valley Civilization with distant regions, including Mesopotamia and Central Asia.
• Artifacts such as seals, pottery, and raw materials found at distant sites provide clues about the commodities exchanged and the nature of these interactions.
• The discoveries shed light on the sophisticated economic organization and maritime capabilities of the Harappan people.
• These findings contribute to a deeper understanding of the Harappan civilization's influence and its integration into early global trade systems.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Ancient Indian history
Indus Valley Civilization
GS1.source: Archaeological Survey of India / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: The Indus Valley Civilization (also known as Harappan Civilization) flourished around 2500-1900 BCE.
Major Harappan sites include Harappa, Mohenjo-Daro, Lothal, Dholavira, and Rakhigarhi.
Mesopotamia was a contemporary civilization with which the Harappans had trade relations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in re-interpreting the economic and cultural interactions of the Indus Valley Civilization with other ancient civilizations.
Analyze the features of Harappan trade networks, including commodities exchanged, modes of transport, and the role of specific sites like Lothal.
Examine how archaeological evidence helps reconstruct the social, economic, and political structures of ancient civilizations like the Harappan.
GS1.imageDescription: Reconstruction of a Harappan city with trade boats in a dockyard.
GS1.trivia: Lothal, located in Gujarat, is believed to have been a major port city of the Indus Valley Civilization, featuring a well-planned dockyard.

GS2.headline: Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha Witness Heated Debate on 'Operation Sindoor' Amidst Opposition Protests on Bihar's Special Intensive Rolls Revision
GS2.explainer:
• Parliamentarians engaged in a special discussion regarding 'Operation Sindoor' and its implications for the electoral process.
• Opposition parties staged protests, expressing concerns over alleged irregularities and political motivations behind the Special Intensive Rolls Revision in Bihar.
• The debate highlighted issues of federalism, electoral integrity, and the role of central government interventions in state-level electoral administration.
• Concerns were raised about the transparency and fairness of the voter registration process, potentially disenfranchising segments of the population.
• The government defended the operation as a necessary measure to ensure accurate and updated voter lists, crucial for democratic elections.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures
Government policies and interventions
Federalism
Electoral reforms
Issues arising out of design and implementation of policies
prelimsPoints: Operation Sindoor refers to a specific initiative, reportedly related to electoral roll revision in Bihar.
The discussion occurred simultaneously in both houses of the Indian Parliament: Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.
Special Intensive Rolls Revision is a process to update and verify voter lists.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges of maintaining electoral integrity in India, especially concerning voter roll revisions and potential political interference.
Analyze the role of parliamentary oversight and opposition protests in holding the government accountable for policies affecting democratic processes.
Examine the delicate balance of federal relations when central initiatives intersect with state administration of elections.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of the Indian Parliament building with protesting politicians outside
GS2.trivia: The Election Commission of India is an autonomous constitutional authority responsible for administering election processes in India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges

GS2.headline: Hindi vs. Marathi: Thackeray Factions Unite on Language Protection, Signaling Maharashtra's Shifting Political Landscape
GS2.explainer:
• Uddhav Thackeray and Raj Thackeray shared a public stage after 19 years, brought together by concerns over the perceived threat to Marathi language and culture.
• The reunion was reportedly prompted by the BJP's language experiments and policies, seen as promoting Hindi at the expense of regional languages.
• This unexpected unity could reshape political alliances in Maharashtra, impacting upcoming state and national elections.
• Language politics continues to be a potent force in Indian federalism, often triggering regional sentiments and political realignments.
• Deputy Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis's actions were cited as a catalyst for the Thackerays to overcome their long-standing differences.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Regionalism
Political parties and pressure groups
prelimsPoints: Uddhav Thackeray and Raj Thackeray are prominent political figures in Maharashtra, leading different factions of the Shiv Sena.
The event marks their first shared public platform in 19 years.
The issue at hand is the protection of the Marathi language and culture against alleged Hindi imposition.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of language in shaping regional identities and political movements in India, using the Thackeray reunion as a case study.
Discuss the challenges posed by linguistic nationalism to India's federal structure and national unity.
Evaluate the impact of such political realignments on state-level governance and the broader national political landscape.
GS2.imageDescription: Uddhav Thackeray and Raj Thackeray shaking hands on a stage with a Marathi flag in the background.
GS2.trivia: Maharashtra Day, celebrated on May 1st, commemorates the formation of the state of Maharashtra from the erstwhile Bombay State on linguistic grounds.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure

GS2.headline: Netanyahu’s Military Successes Come at a Dangerous Cost: Israel’s Unchecked Aggression Deepens Regional Fault Lines and Its Own Vulnerabilities
GS2.explainer:
• Israel's recent military operations, while achieving tactical successes, have exacerbated existing tensions across the Middle East.
• Critics argue that the aggressive stance has deepened regional fault lines, making long-term peace and stability more elusive.
• The article highlights that Israel's unchecked aggression is paradoxically increasing its own vulnerabilities, potentially leading to prolonged conflict.
• The humanitarian cost and displacement of populations in conflict zones have drawn widespread international condemnation.
• Regional powers and international bodies express concern over the escalating violence and its potential to destabilize the broader geopolitical landscape.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora
Conflict zones and their resolution
prelimsPoints: Benjamin Netanyahu is the Prime Minister of Israel.
The article refers to recent military successes and their long-term regional impact.
The Middle East is a significant region for global geopolitical stability.
mainsPoints: Analyze how Israel's military strategies, while achieving short-term objectives, might be counterproductive to long-term regional stability and its own security interests.
Discuss the role of international bodies and global powers in mediating conflicts in the Middle East and the challenges they face.
Examine the implications of escalating conflicts in West Asia for India's foreign policy and energy security.
GS2.imageDescription: Map of the Middle East showing conflict zones and regional powers.
GS2.trivia: The term 'Middle East' gained prominence in the early 20th century, largely replacing the 'Near East' to describe the region.

GS2.headline: Israel’s ‘Total War’ Wrecks the Axis of Resistance but Not Its Logic; Lasting Peace Demands a Viable Palestinian State
GS2.explainer:
• Israel's recent 'total war' strategy has significantly disrupted the operational capabilities of various groups constituting the 'Axis of Resistance'.
• Despite military setbacks, the underlying ideological logic and shared goals of these groups are expected to reconfigure and persist.
• The article argues that a lasting and sustainable peace in the region is contingent upon the establishment of a viable Palestinian state.
• Iran and allied groups are predicted to adapt through informal economies and renewed ideological solidarity, rather than being dismantled.
• International efforts for a two-state solution are highlighted as crucial, with current military actions perceived as delaying rather than resolving the core issue.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
India and its neighbourhood- relations
Conflict zones and their resolution
prelimsPoints: The 'Axis of Resistance' is a loose alliance of states and non-state actors in the Middle East, led by Iran, opposing Israeli and Western influence.
A viable Palestinian state is a key component of the proposed 'two-state solution' for the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.
Iran plays a central role in supporting several groups within the Axis of Resistance.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of military approaches in resolving deeply entrenched ideological conflicts, specifically in the context of the Israeli-Palestinian issue.
Discuss the concept of the 'Axis of Resistance' and its evolving nature in response to geopolitical pressures and military actions.
Argue for the imperative of a viable Palestinian state in achieving lasting peace and stability in the Middle East, considering various international perspectives.
GS2.imageDescription: Stylized map showing the influence of the 'Axis of Resistance' in the Middle East.
GS2.trivia: The concept of a 'two-state solution' emerged prominently after the 1967 Six-Day War, advocating for an independent Palestinian state alongside Israel.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Conflict resolution and international peace efforts

GS2.headline: Israel’s 12-Day War Leaves Iran on a Security-First Path, Accelerating Shift from Revolutionary Idealism to Strategic Pragmatism
GS2.explainer:
• The recent 12-day conflict involving Israel has significantly impacted Iran's foreign policy calculus and internal dynamics.
• The war has deepened Iran’s international isolation, forcing a re-evaluation of its regional and global strategies.
• Internally, the conflict has revived a strong nationalist core within Iran, potentially shifting focus from revolutionary ideals to national security.
• This shift indicates a move towards strategic pragmatism, prioritizing national interests and security over ideological expansionism.
• The article suggests that Iran's future actions will be more calculated and defensive, aiming to consolidate power and protect its borders amidst regional volatility.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
India and its neighbourhood- relations
Geopolitics of West Asia
prelimsPoints: Iran's foreign policy has historically been influenced by its revolutionary ideals since 1979.
The article discusses a shift towards 'strategic pragmatism' in Iran's foreign policy.
The 12-day war refers to a specific recent conflict involving Israel, impacting Iran.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors driving Iran's shift from revolutionary idealism to strategic pragmatism in its foreign policy, and its implications for regional stability.
Discuss how external conflicts and international isolation can reshape a nation's internal political dynamics and foster nationalism.
Examine the potential impact of Iran's evolving foreign policy on India's strategic interests, particularly in energy security and regional connectivity.
GS2.imageDescription: Iranian flag alongside a map of the Gulf region, symbolizing its strategic focus.
GS2.trivia: The Iranian Revolution of 1979 transformed Iran from a monarchy into an Islamic republic, profoundly impacting its geopolitical stance.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Foreign policy shifts of key regional players

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Directs Government to Expedite Rules for Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Emphasizing Citizen Privacy Rights
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has issued directives to the Union Government regarding the swift formulation and notification of rules under the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023.
• The ruling underscores the judiciary's commitment to safeguarding citizen privacy rights in the digital age.
• Delays in framing rules have created a regulatory vacuum, impacting data fiduciaries and data principals across various sectors.
• The court emphasized the need for clarity and enforceability to effectively implement the provisions of the DPDP Act.
• This decision is crucial for protecting individuals' personal data and fostering trust in India's digital economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Role of Supreme Court
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is India's comprehensive law on data privacy.
The Supreme Court's directives are aimed at ensuring timely implementation of the Act.
The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy judgment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, in the context of India's growing digital economy and the fundamental right to privacy.
Analyze the challenges involved in framing and implementing rules for complex legislation like data protection, considering stakeholder interests and technological advancements.
Examine the role of the judiciary in ensuring the timely and effective implementation of laws and protecting citizen rights against executive delays.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building facade, symbolizing judicial intervention.
GS2.trivia: The concept of a separate data protection law for India gained prominence after the Justice B.N. Srikrishna Committee's recommendations in 2018.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and data protection laws

GS2.headline: India Gears Up for Upcoming India-Africa Forum Summit, Focusing on Enhanced Cooperation in Trade, Technology, and Capacity Building
GS2.explainer:
• Preparations are underway for the next India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS), signaling India's continued strategic outreach to the African continent.
• The summit aims to bolster cooperation across key sectors including trade, investment, technology transfer, and human resource development.
• India emphasizes a partnership approach based on mutual respect, shared growth, and South-South cooperation, distinct from traditional donor-recipient models.
• Discussions will likely cover global issues such as climate change, counter-terrorism, and reforms of multilateral institutions.
• The summit underscores Africa's growing geopolitical and economic importance and India's commitment to strengthening ties with African nations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: The India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) is the official platform for India-Africa relations.
Key pillars of cooperation often include trade, investment, technology, and human resource development.
India's engagement with Africa is based on a model of South-South cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of India's engagement with Africa, outlining the key areas of cooperation and its 'South-South' approach.
Discuss how the India-Africa Forum Summit contributes to India's foreign policy objectives and its aspiration for a greater role in global governance.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in deepening India-Africa relations, considering geopolitical shifts and the presence of other global powers in Africa.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and various African nations in a conference setting.
GS2.trivia: The first India-Africa Forum Summit was held in New Delhi in 2008, marking a new era of strategic partnership.

GS2.headline: Government Reviews Progress on National Mental Healthcare Programme, Addressing Gaps in Access and Stigma Reduction
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare conducted a review of the National Mental Healthcare Programme (NMHP) to assess its implementation.
• The review identified persistent gaps in the accessibility of mental health services, particularly in rural and remote areas.
• Efforts to reduce the pervasive social stigma associated with mental illness were highlighted as crucial for encouraging help-seeking behavior.
• The government plans to strengthen infrastructure, increase the workforce of mental health professionals, and integrate mental health into primary healthcare.
• This initiative is vital for addressing the growing burden of mental health disorders and ensuring holistic well-being for all citizens.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: The National Mental Healthcare Programme (NMHP) was launched in 1982 to provide mental health services.
The Mental Healthcare Act, 2017, decriminalized suicide and provides for rights of persons with mental illness.
Integration of mental health services into primary healthcare is a key strategy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in providing comprehensive mental healthcare in India, focusing on issues of access, affordability, and social stigma.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives like the National Mental Healthcare Programme and the Mental Healthcare Act, 2017, in addressing mental health concerns.
Suggest innovative strategies and policy recommendations for strengthening India's mental healthcare infrastructure and promoting mental well-being across all sections of society.
GS2.imageDescription: Symbolic image of a brain with intertwined hands, representing mental health support.
GS2.trivia: Globally, one in eight people live with a mental disorder, and India accounts for a significant portion of this burden.

GS2.headline: Smart Cities Mission Enters New Phase, Focusing on Sustainable Urban Planning, Climate Resilience, and Citizen-Centric Services
GS2.explainer:
• The Smart Cities Mission (SCM) has entered its next phase, building upon the lessons learned from the initial implementation.
• The renewed focus is on integrating sustainable urban planning principles, emphasizing green infrastructure and resource efficiency.
• Climate resilience is a major theme, with projects aimed at mitigating and adapting to the impacts of climate change in urban areas.
• Emphasis is placed on delivering citizen-centric services through technology, improving quality of life and urban governance.
• The mission seeks to transform urban landscapes into more livable, economically vibrant, and environmentally sustainable spaces.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
GS2.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: The Smart Cities Mission was launched by the Indian government in 2015.
Its objective is to promote cities that provide core infrastructure, a clean and sustainable environment, and a decent quality of life to their citizens.
The mission covers 100 cities across India.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the progress and challenges of the Smart Cities Mission in India, particularly in its new phase focusing on sustainability and climate resilience.
Discuss how technology and citizen participation can enhance urban governance and improve the delivery of essential services in smart cities.
Examine the role of sustainable urban planning in addressing the growing challenges of urbanization, environmental degradation, and climate change in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A futuristic city skyline with green spaces and smart infrastructure elements.
GS2.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission is based on the concept of 'area-based development', which includes retrofitting, redevelopment, and greenfield development.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Gains Pace with Focus on Foundational Literacy and Numeracy Across States
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education reports significant progress in the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• A key thrust is on strengthening Foundational Literacy and Numeracy (FLN) skills among young learners through various state-level initiatives.
• The policy aims to transform India's education system from early childhood to higher education, promoting holistic and multidisciplinary learning.
• Efforts include curriculum reforms, teacher training programs, and leveraging technology for improved learning outcomes.
• Challenges persist in ensuring equitable access and quality education across diverse socio-economic backgrounds, requiring sustained collaborative efforts.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS2.source: Ministry of Education / The Indian Express
prelimsPoints: The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the 1986 policy.
Foundational Literacy and Numeracy (FLN) is a core component of NEP, aimed at achieving universal FLN by 2025.
The NIPUN Bharat Mission is a national initiative to achieve the goals of FLN.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in addressing the challenges of India's education system.
Analyze the importance of Foundational Literacy and Numeracy (FLN) for overall educational development and its role in achieving NEP's objectives.
Examine the challenges in implementing a nationwide education policy like NEP, considering federal structure, resource allocation, and diverse regional needs.
GS2.imageDescription: Children in a classroom actively engaged in learning activities, symbolizing foundational education.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 recommends a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure, replacing the 10+2 structure.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government policies and programs in education

GS3.headline: National Research Foundation Gains Momentum: Government Announces New Funding Tranches for Interdisciplinary Research and Development
GS3.explainer:
• The government has released fresh tranches of funding for the National Research Foundation (NRF), reinforcing its commitment to scientific advancement.
• These funds are earmarked to promote interdisciplinary research, fostering collaboration across various scientific and technological domains.
• The NRF aims to strengthen India's research ecosystem, driving innovation and enhancing global competitiveness in science and technology.
• Focus areas include emerging technologies, sustainable development, health sciences, and fundamental research.
• The initiative is expected to boost private sector participation and attract top talent to research careers, aligning with the National Education Policy (NEP).
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: The National Research Foundation (NRF) was established to fund and promote research across various disciplines in India.
It aims to bridge the gap between academic research and industrial application.
The NRF operates under the Department of Science & Technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and potential impact of the National Research Foundation (NRF) in transforming India's scientific research landscape.
Analyze the importance of interdisciplinary research in addressing complex national and global challenges, citing examples of NRF's focus areas.
Examine the role of government funding and policy interventions in fostering a robust innovation ecosystem and promoting 'Make in India' in science and technology.
GS3.imageDescription: Graphic representing interdisciplinary research connecting various scientific fields.
